title: Global Energy Dashboard
description: Led a 5-person team at UCSD's Center for Energy Research to build a live dashboard tracking EV market growth and oil import dependency across 50+ countries (1971–2023). Applied ARIMA and logistic S-curve models to generate 10-year energy security forecasts, then presented findings directly to the CER Director to support long-term infrastructure planning.

title: NBA G-League Call-Up Predictor
description: Full-stack prediction system built on an automated SportsRadar API ETL pipeline pulling 5 seasons of G-League player stats, contract logs, and roster data. XGBoost and Logistic Regression models achieve 82% recall on a heavily imbalanced dataset. Deployed as a Streamlit app with interactive simulators and SHAP explainability — model outputs were later validated by an NBA scout.

title: NourishOne — Food Pantry Finder
description: Built in 72 hours at a hackathon: a React + Leaflet food access platform aggregating and geocoding 737 verified food pantry locations across DC, Maryland, and Virginia. I led data engineering — cleaning, validating, and structuring the location dataset so the map interface could filter by food type, dietary needs, distance, and language access.

title: Boston Bike Map
description: Interactive map built with D3.js and Mapbox GL JS to visualize Boston bike-share traffic patterns by time of day and direction. The visualization reveals how station usage inverts between morning and evening commutes, with real-time filtering that shows volume and directional flow simultaneously.

title: Modeling Objective Control in League of Legends
description: Analyzed professional League of Legends match data to model how Baron and Dragon objectives each influence win probability. Built classification models to quantify the predictive power of each objective type and tested whether conventional wisdom about Baron vs. Dragon priority holds up statistically.

title: San Diego Airbnb Pricing Analysis
description: Analyzed San Diego Airbnb listings to identify what drives pricing — location, property type, availability windows, and listing features. Includes data cleaning, exploratory analysis, regression modeling, and visualizations that surface actionable patterns for hosts and platform analysts.

title: Waymo Expansion and SF Transportation Trends
description: Explored how Waymo's driverless ride-hailing expansion in San Francisco correlates with shifts in taxi activity, Muni ridership, and urban transportation demand. Applied time-series analysis to public datasets and built visualizations to identify displacement patterns and modal shift signals across transit modes.
